title: EFASL Appoints New Committee for 2025/26
text: The Eisenhower Fellowships Association of Sri Lanka has appointed its governing committee for the 2025/26 term. Mr. Heminda Jayaweera (EF 2021) has been appointed as Chairman. Committee members include Ms. Seroshi Nandasiri (EF 2024), Mr. Asiri Hewamalage (EF 2024), and Ms. Nevindaree Premarathne (EF 2025). The association looks forward to a productive term under this new leadership.

title: New Interim Committee Appointed at EFASL AGM 2017
text: At the Annual General Meeting held on December 07, 2017, a new Interim Committee was appointed to steer the Eisenhower Fellowships Association of Sri Lanka. Mr. J.C. Weliamuna (EF 2004 SARP) was appointed as Chairman, with Mr. Ruwanthie de Chikera (EF 2014 MNP) as Secretary and Mr. Jayantha Fernando (EF 2012 SARP) continuing as Treasurer. Mr. Harsha Purasinghe (EF 2014 MNP) was appointed to the newly created role of Secretary Innovation, reflecting the association's growing focus on technology and innovation. The committee also welcomes Mr. Dayananda Gunawardena (EF 1986 MNP) and Mr. Nayana Mawilmada (EF 2017 MESA) as committee members. The association looks forward to a productive term under this new leadership.

title: ESASL AGM 2013 Elects New Committee for 2013/14
text: The Annual General Meeting held on November 30, 2013 at Colombo elected a new governing committee for the 2013/14 term. Dr. Paikiasothy Saravanamuttu (2004 SRP) was elected as Chairman, with Mr. Saliya Peiries (2012 SRP) as Secretary and Mr. Jayantha Fernando (2012 SRP) continuing as Treasurer. The committee also includes Mr. Chrishantha Weliamuna, Mrs. Susie Perera, and Mrs. Kanchana Weerakoon as committee members. The outgoing leadership of Mr. Chandra Jayaratne (Past Chairman) and Mr. Dayananda Gunawardena (Past Secretary and Treasurer) were warmly recognised for their contributions to the association.

title: EFASL Establishes Governing Committee for 2008/09
text: The Eisenhower Fellowships Association of Sri Lanka appointed its governing committee for the 2008/09 term. Mr. Chandra Jayaratne (EF 1989 MNP) was appointed Chairman, and Mr. H. Dayananda Gunawardena (EF 1986 MNP) took on the combined role of Secretary and Treasurer. Committee members included Mr. Chrishantha Weliamuna (2004 SRP), Mrs. Kala Peiris De Costa (2004 SRP), and Mrs. Subashini Rasanja (2007 MNP). The committee continued to build on the association's foundation of fostering leadership, international networking, and knowledge sharing among Eisenhower Fellows in Sri Lanka.

title: Tech Entrepreneur Drives Social Impact
text: As a young technology-focused entrepreneur, Harsha Purasinghe (Sri Lanka 2014) was instrumental in initiating and leading a unique social innovation in the aftermath of an Indian Ocean tsunami in 2004.

title: On the Road with George and Carrie
text: EF President George de Lama and his wife Carrie Holt de Lama have been on the move over the last few weeks, visiting eight countries in February and March to meet with Fellows, Trustees, donors and other friends of EF from Taipei to Istanbul.

title: Sri Lanka’s biggest online retailer takes more slices of the “On-Demand” Economy
EF Fellow - Jayantha Fernando honored with LifeTime Award
Humanitarian Relief in Sri Lanka
Eran picked for finance, Harsha for economic affairs
The eisenhower day celebrations conference 2016
Stephanie's visit to Colombo
text: Ms. Stephanie Veit (EF Executive Director- Advancement and External Relations) visited Sri Lanka in June this year. The visit kicked off with meetings at the Sri Lankan parliament, where she had discussions with Hon. Harsha de Silva (Deputy Minister of Policy Planning and Economic Development, EF MNP 2011) as well as Hon. Eran Wickramaratne (Deputy Minister of Highways, Education and Investment Promotion, EF SRP 2004). Stephanie also spent time at the US Embassy, where she met Senior Officers to discuss how to expand the EF program in the region. She was hosted by Nathan Sivagananathan (EF MNP 2015) for an evening of fellowship with the Eisenhower Alumni network in Sri Lanka, which was also attended by Mr. Zeeshan Usmani (EF 2012) and his team from Predictifyme. Stephanie’s visit concluded with a visit to the historical town of Galle, where she spent the day enjoying some sun, sand and cultural heritage before returning to the United States. It was a pleasure to have Stephanie in Sri Lanka and we look forward to seeing her here again soon.

title: BRIGITTE DANIEL - 2011 USA EISENHOWER FELLOW
2002 Berlin Germany Eisenhower Fellowships Conference
Youth Leadership Program 2026 – Second Cohort Launched
text:
We are proud to announce the launch of the second cohort of the Youth Leadership Program on the 19th of May 2026 at TRACE Expert City, Colombo. This year, we have selected 15 exceptional young leaders representing diverse backgrounds, regions, and communities across Sri Lanka, all united by a shared commitment to driving positive change and creating meaningful impact. These young changemakers embody the spirit of leadership, innovation, inclusion, and service that our nation needs for the future.

As we begin this journey together, we are inspired by the passion, resilience, and vision of this cohort, who are already championing transformation within their communities and beyond.

We extend our sincere gratitude to Eisenhower Fellowships for their continued support and belief in empowering the next generation of leaders, and to all Sri Lankan Eisenhower Fellows for their unwavering commitment, mentorship, and support in making this initiative possible.

Together, we continue to invest in leadership that connects, inspires, and transforms Sri Lanka for generations to come.

title: Inaugural Youth Leadership Program 2025 Concludes Successfully
text:
We successfully concluded the inaugural Youth Leadership Program 2025 on Friday, 17th October, at Trace Expert City, Bay 12, Colombo. Eight exceptional young leaders completed this transformative journey, marking the beginning of a new generation of purpose-driven changemakers.

We were deeply honored to have Senior Eisenhower Fellows join the occasion, sharing their wisdom and inspiration. Dulith Herath and Bhavani Fonseka delivered powerful reflections on their Eisenhower Fellowship experiences, leaving the youth leaders with invaluable lessons on leadership, courage, and impact.

This milestone reaffirms our commitment to nurturing the next generation of resilient, ethical, and visionary leaders for Sri Lanka and beyond.
